ucs.title: [LLM] RansomHub EDRKillShifter BYOVD driver-load to terminate EDR
ucs.description: Hunts for the EDRKillShifter loader and its signed-but-vulnerable drivers (e.g., RentDrv2.sys, TrueSight.sys, ThreatFireMonitor.sys) that RansomHub affiliates run pre-encryption to kill EDR/AV. The combo of an unsigned user-mode loader dropping a signed kernel driver into a non-standard path, then registering it as a service, is RansomHub-characteristic and was specifically called out by Sophos X-Ops, CrowdStrike, and CISA AA24-242A.
ucs.splunk:
| tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.process_path) as process_path from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN ("EDRKillShifter.exe","loader.exe","killer.exe","shifter.exe") OR (Processes.process="*config.txt*" AND Processes.process="*pass*") OR Processes.process IN ("*RentDrv2.sys*","*TrueSight.sys*","*ThreatFireMonitor.sys*","*viragt64.sys*","*WnBios.sys*","*aswArPot.sys*") by host Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name Processes.process_id _time 
| `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` 
| join type=outer host [| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where (Filesystem.file_name IN ("RentDrv2.sys","TrueSight.sys","ThreatFireMonitor.sys","viragt64.sys","WnBios.sys","aswArPot.sys") AND Filesystem.file_path!="*\\System32\\drivers\\*") by host Filesystem.file_path Filesystem.file_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)`] 
| join type=outer host [| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Endpoint.Registry where Registry.registry_path="*\\Services\\*" AND Registry.registry_value_name="ImagePath" AND Registry.registry_value_data IN ("*RentDrv*","*TrueSight*","*ThreatFireMonitor*","*viragt64*") by host Registry.registry_key_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Registry)`] 
| where isnotnull(file_name) OR isnotnull(registry_key_name) OR match(process_name,"(?i)edrkillshifter") 
| sort - lastTime
ucs.kql:
let vulnDrivers = dynamic(["rentdrv2.sys","truesight.sys","threatfiremonitor.sys","viragt64.sys","wnbios.sys","aswarpot.sys","dbutil_2_3.sys"]);
let loaderNames = dynamic(["edrkillshifter.exe","shifter.exe","killer.exe","loader.exe"]);
let driverDrops =
    DeviceFileEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where FileName has_any (vulnDrivers)
    | where FolderPath !has @"\System32\drivers\" and FolderPath !has @"\SysWOW64\drivers\"
    | project Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, DroppedDriver=FileName, DropPath=FolderPath, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessAccountName;
let loaderExec =
    DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where FileName in~ (loaderNames)
       or (ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\.exe\s+[A-Fa-f0-9]{32,}\b")  // hex passphrase arg pattern
       or ProcessCommandLine has_all ("config",".txt")
       or ProcessCommandLine has_any (vulnDrivers)
    | project Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, ProcLoader=FileName, LoaderPath=FolderPath, ProcessCommandLine, AccountName, ParentProcessFileName;
let svcInstall =
    DeviceRegistryEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where RegistryKey has @"\System\CurrentControlSet\Services\"
    | where RegistryValueName == "ImagePath"
    | where RegistryValueData has_any (vulnDrivers)
    | project Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, RegistryKey, RegistryValueData;
driverDrops
| join kind=inner (loaderExec) on DeviceId
| join kind=leftouter (svcInstall) on DeviceId
| where abs(datetime_diff('minute', Timestamp, Timestamp1)) < 30
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, ProcLoader, LoaderPath, ProcessCommandLine, DroppedDriver, DropPath, RegistryValueData
ucs.rationale: EDRKillShifter is a near-unique RansomHub affiliate tool — Sophos first attributed it to a RansomHub intrusion in mid-2024, and CISA AA24-242A reiterated it. The detection chains three observable artefacts that legitimate admins almost never produce together: (1) a loader binary executing with a hex passphrase argument or config.txt, (2) a known vulnerable signed driver dropped outside %SystemRoot

ucs.title: [LLM] RansomHub pre-encryption rclone exfil to MEGA/S3 followed by VSS deletion
ucs.description: Hunts the RansomHub data-theft-then-encrypt sequence: affiliates routinely stage data with rclone.exe (often renamed) pushing to mega.nz, backblaze, or S3, then immediately invoke vssadmin/wmic to delete shadow copies and stop services before the Linux/Windows encryptor runs. CISA AA24-242A and Mandiant track this exact rclone-to-MEGA → vssadmin delete shadows → bcdedit chain as RansomHub TTPs.
ucs.splunk:
| tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdlines values(Processes.process_name) as procs from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process IN ("*rclone*","*\\svchost.exe* copy *remote:*","* copy * mega:*","* sync * --transfers*","*config=* --no-check-certificate*") OR Processes.process IN ("*vssadmin* delete shadows*","*wmic* shadowcopy delete*","*bcdedit* /set*recoveryenabled* No*","*bcdedit* /set*bootstatuspolicy* ignoreallfailures*","*wevtutil* cl *","*net stop*","*sc* stop*","*Stop-Service*")) by host Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.parent_process_name _time span=1h 
| `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` 
| stats values(procs) as procs values(cmdlines) as cmdlines dc(eval(case(match(cmdlines,"(?i)rclone|mega:|backblaze|s3:|onedrive:|--transfers"),"exfil",match(cmdlines,"(?i)vssadmin|shadowcopy|bcdedit|wevtutil"),"recovery",match(cmdlines,"(?i)net stop|sc stop|Stop-Service"),"svcstop"))) as stages min(firstTime) as firstTime max(lastTime) as lastTime by host user 
| where stages>=2 
| eval window_minutes=round((lastTime-firstTime)/60,0) 
| where window_minutes <= 240 
| `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)` 
| sort - lastTime
ucs.kql:
let exfilWindow = 4h;
let rcloneSigns = dynamic(["rclone","--config"," copy "," sync ","mega:","backblaze:","s3:","onedrive:","--transfers","--no-check-certificate"]);
let recoverySigns = dynamic(["vssadmin delete shadows","wmic shadowcopy delete","bcdedit /set","recoveryenabled no","bootstatuspolicy ignoreallfailures","wevtutil cl"]);
let svcStop = dynamic(["net stop ","sc.exe stop","Stop-Service","taskkill /im sql","taskkill /im veeam","taskkill /im backup"]);
let exfil =
    DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where (FileName =~ "rclone.exe" or InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "rclone.exe")
         or ProcessCommandLine has_any (rcloneSigns)
    | where ProcessCommandLine has_any (dynamic(["mega:","backblaze:","s3:","onedrive:","gdrive:","dropbox:","--transfers","copy ","sync "]))
    | project ExfilTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, ExfilCmd=ProcessCommandLine, ExfilUser=AccountName, ExfilProc=FileName;
let recovery =
    DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where ProcessCommandLine has_any (recoverySigns)
    | project RecTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, RecCmd=ProcessCommandLine;
let stops =
    DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where ProcessCommandLine has_any (svcStop)
    | where ProcessCommandLine has_any (dynamic(["sql","veeam","backup","exchange","vss","sophos","defender","mssql"]))
    | project StopTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, StopCmd=ProcessCommandLine;
exfil
| join kind=inner (recovery) on DeviceId
| where datetime_diff('minute', RecTime, ExfilTime) between (-240 .. 240)
| join kind=leftouter (stops) on DeviceId
| summarize FirstSeen=min(ExfilTime), LastSeen=max(RecTime),
            ExfilCommands=make_set(ExfilCmd,5),
            RecoveryCommands=make_set(RecCmd,5),
            ServiceStopCommands=make_set(StopCmd,5),
            User=any(ExfilUser)
            by DeviceName, DeviceId
| extend Stages = iff(array_length(ServiceStopCommands)>0, 3, 2)
| where Stages >= 2
| order by LastSeen desc
ucs.rationale: The rclone-to-cloud + shadow-copy-deletion + service-stop trio in a <4h window is the canonical RansomHub double-extortion playbook documented in CISA AA24-242A, Symantec, and Mandiant reporting. Individually each command (vssadmin delete, net stop, rclone) can be benign or noisy, but their co-occurrence on a single host within hours is essentially diagnostic of human-operated ransomware staging —
